38: **Spain** arrive in dominant form and carry the quality to dismantle most group-stage opposition — **Saudi Arabia** will need to be disciplined, compact, and clinical on the counter to stand any chance. La Roja's midfield control and attacking depth make them heavy favorites, but the Saudis showed at the last edition of the finals that they can produce a historic result when the moment calls for it.
39: **Belgium**'s Golden Generation may have faded, but a new-look squad still carries real threat — and they cannot afford a slip-up against a resilient **IR Iran** side built on defensive structure and collective grit. The Iranians will look to frustrate, absorb, and punish on the break, making this a potential banana skin for the Red Devils if they can't break down a low block early.
37: **Uruguay** should have too much experience and attacking quality for **Cabo Verde**, but La Celeste have a habit of making easy games uncomfortable. The Blue Sharks are a well-drilled, physically committed side who will press and harass — **Uruguay** need to be patient, control the tempo, and let Darwin Núñez and company do their work in the final third.
40: **New Zealand** and **Egypt** meet in a match that could define Group G's bottom half, with both sides desperate to avoid back-to-back losses. The Pharaohs carry more individual quality and were the better team in their opener, but the All Whites' work rate and organization at this stage should never be underestimated. Expect a cagey, physical contest where set pieces and transitions decide the outcome.
34: **Ecuador** are strong favorites against tournament debutants **Curaçao**, the smallest nation ever to reach this stage. La Tri's miserly defense was among the stingiest in qualifying, and they'll look to control the game and ease toward the knockouts — while **Curaçao**, with nothing to lose, will sit deep and hunt a famous counter-attacking moment.
36: **Japan**, one of Asia's most polished and well-organized sides, meet a physical **Tunisia** team that thrives on set pieces and defensive discipline. The Samurai Blue's quick, technical passing will test the Carthage Eagles, who'll look to slow the tempo and strike on the break — and after **Netherlands**' five-goal showing earlier today, both sides know exactly what a loss here could cost them in Group F.
